findings.description: The title slide background image/gradient is rendered as a small, cropped banner image in the HTML rather than as a full-page slide background.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a full-page blue gradient slide with title text overlaid. HTML shows a small rectangular banner image at top with text below it as plain HTML headings.

findings.description: Flow Components slide contains two detailed diagrams (hydrograph and cross-section illustration) that appear in the HTML but are split across page boundaries and may lose context.
findings.evidence: PDF page 5 shows both diagrams together on one slide. HTML splits them across sections, though both images are present.

findings.description: The Cheoah River example slide image/video placeholder is rendered as a black rectangle in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF slide 7 shows a table with data for Cheoah River. HTML shows a large black rectangle placeholder above the table.

findings.description: Trinity River example chart image is partially visible in HTML, cut off at page boundary.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the full Trinity River hydrograph chart on one slide. HTML rendering shows the chart but it appears split between content sections.

findings.description: The Flow Regime Tied to Ecology slide has a structured flowchart/organizational diagram that is converted to a flat bulleted list in HTML, losing the hierarchical visual structure.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a flowchart with 'Sound Ecological Environment' at top branching into 4 flow types, each with sub-boxes. HTML renders this as nested bullet points.

findings.description: Cheoah River high flow events table (Table 2) is present and structurally correct in HTML but the footnote superscript formatting differs slightly.
findings.evidence: PDF shows superscript numbers (1, 2, 3) within the table. HTML shows them as superscript text with footnotes below, which is functionally equivalent.

findings.description: Multiple h1 headings exist. The title page content uses h1 for 'Introduction to Flow Scenarios', but 'Instream Flow Status' on page 2 is rendered as h2 while the same heading on page 3 is also h2 — creating duplicate h2 headings with the same text. More importantly, the HTML has nested <main> elements, which is a structural issue.
findings.evidence: Two <main> elements are nested. The h1 'Introduction to Flow Scenarios' is correct as the single h1, and subsequent slides are h2, which is acceptable. The duplicate h2 'Instream Flow Status (pp 84-86 of IFC book)' appears twice matching the PDF's two slides with the same title — this is not an error per se.

findings.description: On page 10, 'Sound Ecological Environment' is rendered as h3 under 'Flow Regime Tied to Ecology' h2. In the PDF, 'Sound Ecological Environment' is the central box in a diagram/flowchart, not a traditional subheading. This is an acceptable structural decision but slightly misrepresents the diagram as a text hierarchy.
findings.evidence:
PDF page 10 shows 'Sound Ecological Environment' as a central box in a flowchart diagram. HTML renders it as '

Sound Ecological Environment

'.

findings.description: The Cheoah River base flow table on page 8 is represented both as an image with alt text referencing a 'Table graphic' and as an actual HTML table. This is a duplication — the same data appears twice.
findings.evidence: HTML page-8 section contains: '' followed by the full HTML with identical data.

findings.description: The HTML includes a comment '<!-- COLUMNS: Tier | Jan | Feb | ... -->' in page 8 and '<!-- COLUMNS: High Flows | ... -->' in page 9. These are metadata comments not present in the PDF, but they are HTML comments, not visible content, so this is very minor.
findings.evidence: HTML comments like '<!-- COLUMNS: Tier | Jan | Feb | Mar | Apr | May | Jun | Jul | Aug | Sep | Oct | Nov | Dec -->' appear in the source.
